name: Metroid
description:
Metroid is a side-scrolling action-adventure game focused on exploration and progression. The player controls Samus Aran, a bounty hunter, as she explores the labyrinthine depths of the planet Zebes. The environment is a vast, interconnected world composed of multiple regions, each with its own unique atmosphere, enemies, and challenges. The primary objective is to defeat Mother Brain, the leader of the Space Pirates, who are using Zebes as their base of operations. 

Gameplay revolves around navigating the environment, battling hostile alien creatures, and discovering power-ups that enhance Samus's abilities. These power-ups unlock new areas of the map and grant her access to previously inaccessible locations. Progression is largely non-linear, encouraging players to explore and backtrack as they acquire new abilities. The game utilizes a map system to aid navigation, allowing players to track their progress and identify areas of interest. The planet is filled with hidden passages, secret rooms, and environmental puzzles that require careful observation and experimentation to overcome. Samus begins with limited capabilities and gradually becomes more powerful as she collects upgrades such as the Morph Ball (allowing her to roll through tight spaces), missiles, and various beam weapons that enhance her combat effectiveness, and the High Jump and Space Jump that allow her to jump higher and move through the air more effectively. The overall atmosphere is one of isolation and tension, emphasizing the feeling of being alone on a hostile alien world.

controls.arrow_up: Aim upwards / Look up when stationary
controls.arrow_down: Crouch / Morph into Ball (when morph ball is acquired)
controls.shift: Hold to run (when speed booster is acquired)
